# Terminal Audio Player

A work-in-progress lightweight desktop audio player written in C using [raylib](https://www.raylib.com/).
Features a directory browser, music playback, and a real-time waveform visualizer using Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT).

## Features

- **Directory browser** — lists music files (`.mp3`) in the current working directory
- **Music playback** — load and play tracks using raylib audio streaming
- **vim keybindings** - (`h`, `j`, `k`, `l`) to move between panes and files
- **Live audio visualizer** — computes DFT on audio samples and renders the frequency spectrum as bars (rectangles)

## Project Structure

```
.
├── audio_player.c          # Main app: window/UI/state management, audio playback
├── math_visualization.c    # DFT implementation used to drive the visualizer
├── math_visualization.h    # Header for the DFT function
└── README.md
```

## Dependencies

- [raylib](https://www.raylib.com/) (graphics/audio/input handling)
- Standard C library (`stdio.h`, `stdlib.h`, `string.h`, `dirent.h`, `complex.h`, etc.)
- A C compiler (GCC/Clang)

On Linux/macOS you need `termios.h`/`fcntl.h` support (used for non-Windows keyboard handling); on Windows, `conio.h` is used instead.

## Building

Assuming raylib is installed on your system (via package manager or built from source):

```bash
gcc audio_player.c math_visualization.c -o audio_player \
    -lraylib -lGL -lm -lpthread -ldl -lrt -lX11
```

> Flags may vary depending on your OS and how raylib was installed. On macOS, you'll typically link against `-framework OpenGL -framework Cocoa -framework IOKit`. On Windows, consult the raylib build instructions for MinGW/MSVC. Run program at your discretion, have fun :)))

## Running

Place one or more `.mp3` files in the same directory as the executable (or the directory you run it from), then:

```bash
./audio_player
```

The app opens a `1500x600` window titled **"Audio Player"**.

## Controls

| Key       | Context               | Action                                             |
|-----------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `H`       | Right file pane        | Enter the left navigation pane                      |
| `L`       | Left pane               | Select the highlighted menu item (enter its pane)    |
| `J`       | Right file pane        | Move selection down in the file list                 |
| `K`       | Right file pane        | Move selection up in the file list                    |
| `J`       | Left pane               | Move highlight down (Directory → Now Playing → Visualizer) |
| `K`       | Left pane               | Move highlight up (Visualizer → Now Playing → Directory)   |
| `Enter`   | Right file pane        | Load and play the selected `.mp3` file               |
| `Esc`/close | Anywhere              | Exit the application                                  |

The left pane has three sections: **Directory**, **Now Playing**, and **Visualizer**. Navigating into "Visualizer" renders a live bar-graph spectrum computed via a Discrete Fourier Transform on the current audio buffer.

## How the Visualizer Works

`AttachAudioStreamProcessor` hooks into the music stream and fills a `frameArray` buffer by averaging the left/right channels of each audio frame. When the Visualizer pane is active, `discreteFourierTransform()` (in `math_visualization.c`) computes the DFT of that buffer and draws each frequency bin as a vertical red bar, giving a real-time frequency-domain view of the currently playing audio. For more details review Discrete Fourier Transform on wikipedia.

## Known Limitations / TODO
